Okay, so we just wrote the moment of highest tension. It passed the neck cage test, it gave you some visceral feelings of discomfort, and now we are going to immediately remedy that and give you some contrast through what I call the moment of highest pleasure. Now at a high level, this is the vivid three-dimensional, sensual experience where everything is okay, restored, brought back to equilibrium, and there really is this sense or energy of redemption. What you're coaching here is possibility, desire, buy-in and commitment to a new future, and trust once again by accurately reflecting back and validating their dreams as well as continued discord and intolerance with the current situation. You know, we're really amplifying the friction through that contrast between their actual reality and what should be their reality. So there's that word again, should, based on who they are, what they've achieved, and other aspects of that exclusive empowerment, what their reality should be. So the moment of highest pleasure is really their birthright. It's what life should be, not could, but the birthright, they're exclusively in power-to-enjoy based on who they are, what they've done, and all those other factors. So one way to really enter it is to say as a blank who has blank, you should be able to blank. Now this is an important point that I can't stress enough, and I know we keep drilling it in, but as we're about to write the moment of highest pleasure, it's worth a reminder. This is not a nice to have, it's what should be based on their achievements, status, abilities, resources, etc. And when you communicate it like that, you're really amplifying their ability and willingness to respond, which is again the very definition of direct response that we're using in the coaching the conversion method. So how are we going to do that? So we've already done a lot of work on this. I'm going to ask you to revisit the Launch Bible workbook in section three, the 67 Golden Questions sections four and section ten, as well as your coach mining enrollment extractor workbook in section two. And the test I want you to leave with to really know you're hitting this is the Andy Dufresne test. Now I love this movie. I think I reference it in every training I ever create, but only because I mean it works. There's a reason it's one of the most famous emotionally compelling movies of all time. And the most emotional beat of the Shawshank Redemption is this image right here with Andy in his, you know, pretty much half naked in the rain, finally achieving that freedom and that redemption. You know, no one really remembers the final frame of him strolling down the Pacific. They remember this shot because it's the moment when an injustice has been corrected when things have gone from not okay to okay, not from okay to amazing. So I invite you to keep this image in mind if you need to while writing your moment of highest pleasure and find what is that redeeming moment, you know, that emotionally gratifying intensely pleasurable moment of pleasure or redemption for your prospect. If you do need more inspiration, as always, we do have the example breakdowns in the next video. But if you are feeling steeped in your prospects moment of highest pleasure and feeling inspired and invigorated to go ahead, stop this video, write it and only go to those examples if you need some extra motivation. So that's all I got for you here. Get started on that moment of highest pleasure. And with that, you will wrap up the first official writing session.
